IRN.fn: This is where Mommy was born. She grew up here, and part of her heart is still in this beautiful place. 💛
IRN.letter: Salaam! Iran is one of the oldest countries in the WHOLE world, and people here invented ice cream over 2,000 years ago. The mountains are covered in snow and the gardens smell like roses. Persian cats with the squishy faces? They come from right here. Iran has the most UNESCO World Heritage Sites in all of Asia. Persian gardens are so legendary that the English word PARADISE comes from the Persian word for garden.

NGA.fn: This is where Daddy's daddy came from. The Oguntala name started right here. 💛
NGA.letter: Hey hey! Nigeria makes MORE movies than Hollywood does, and they call it Nollywood! Families eat jollof rice and EVERYONE argues about whose is best (it's Nigeria's, obviously). More twins are born here than anywhere else on the entire planet. Nigeria's film industry (Nollywood) is the SECOND biggest in the world by number of films, beating Hollywood. Only Bollywood makes more. Lagos is one of the fastest-growing cities on Earth.

GBR.fn: This is where Daddy's family started, a long, long time ago. They crossed the ocean and settled in North Carolina. 💛
GBR.letter: Hiya! Scotland's national animal is a UNICORN. I'm not making that up. Men wear skirts called kilts and if you call it a skirt they'll give you a LOOK. The hills are green and misty and full of hundreds of castles. Loch Ness is 755 feet deep and pitch black. Nobody has ever proven the monster exists, but nobody has proven it DOESN'T exist either. Scotland takes this very seriously.

USA.fn: Daddy's family has lived here for 400 years. They came from Scotland and settled in North Carolina. This is home. 💛
USA.letter: Howdy! This is YOUR country, with 50 states and each one is its own little world. Americans eat 20 BILLION hot dogs every year (that's a lot of mustard). And this is the only country that put actual people on the actual MOON. The Grand Canyon is so enormous that weather happens INSIDE it. It can be snowing at the top and sunny at the bottom at the same time.

IRL.fn: This is part of Daddy's heritage. He's Scots-Irish, with roots that go way back. 💛
IRL.letter: Dia duit! That means hello in Irish. Ireland is called the Emerald Isle because it's SO green. It rains a lot, which means rainbows EVERYWHERE. People here are famous for being great storytellers. Leprechauns, fairies, and pots of gold at the end of the rainbow all come from Irish legends. And Halloween? Ireland invented it.

JPN.letter: Konnichiwa! Japan is where Pokémon was invented. Also Mario. Also Nintendo. Also emoji. You literally use Japanese inventions every single day. Bullet trains go 200 miles per hour and they are NEVER late. If a train is 1 minute late, the company issues an apology. Cherry blossom trees turn the whole country PINK every spring and people have picnics under them. There are vending machines on every corner that sell everything from hot soup to mystery toys. Oh, and there is an island completely taken over by friendly rabbits.

EGY.letter: Ahlan! Egypt built ENORMOUS stone triangles 4,500 years ago and nobody can figure out HOW. Ancient Egyptians loved cats so much they made them into actual GODS (honestly, fair). They did their homework in doodles called hieroglyphics instead of letters. The Great Pyramid was the tallest structure on Earth for almost 4,000 YEARS. Cleopatra lived closer in time to the iPhone than to the building of the pyramids.

BRA.letter: Olá! Brazil has the biggest rainforest on Earth and it's so enormous it makes its OWN rain clouds. Kids here learn to kick a soccer ball before they learn to walk (almost). Carnival is the biggest party in the world, with MILLIONS of people dancing in feathers and glitter. The Amazon produces 20% of the world's oxygen. It is so big it creates its own weather system. There are tribes in the Amazon who have never made contact with the outside world.

MEX.letter: ¡Hola! Mexico is where CHOCOLATE was invented — 3,000 years ago, warriors drank it spicy and bitter. Day of the Dead is a beautiful party with candy skulls and marigold flowers. And the axolotl lives here: a pink smiling salamander that regrows its own LEGS. Mexico City is SINKING because it was built on top of a lake. The cathedral is visibly tilted. Day of the Dead is not scary. It is a beautiful celebration where families honor their loved ones with flowers, food, and music.

AUS.letter: G'day! Australia is so big it's a country AND a continent. Baby kangaroos ride in their mom's pouch like a built-in backpack. When it's winter where YOU live, it's SUMMER here. Also, wombats poop cubes. Real fact. The Great Barrier Reef is so big you can see it from space. It is the largest living structure on Earth. Also, emus and kangaroos cannot walk backwards, which is why they are on the coat of arms (always moving forward).

ITA.letter: Ciao! Italy is shaped like a BOOT. Inside that boot is where pizza was invented — in Naples. First pizza ever: just tomato and cheese. The Colosseum held 50,000 screaming fans. Italian nonnas will feed you until you can't move. Venice is literally SINKING. The city is built on wooden poles hammered into mud, and the water keeps rising. Engineers are fighting to save it.

KEN.letter: Jambo! Kenya is the only place where you can see lions, elephants, and giraffes all before LUNCH. Giraffes are so tall they could peek into your bedroom window on the second floor. Kenyan runners are the fastest humans alive, and scientists STILL can't fully explain why. Kenya's Great Rift Valley is literally the Earth splitting apart in slow motion. In millions of years, East Africa will separate and become its own continent.

FRA.letter: Bonjour! French kids eat chocolate stuffed inside warm, flaky pastry on the way to school. FOR BREAKFAST. The Eiffel Tower was supposed to be torn down but they kept it, and now it's the most famous building on Earth. France has over 1,000 types of cheese, which means you could eat a NEW cheese every day for THREE YEARS.

IND.letter: Namaste! During Holi, everybody throws colored powder at each other until the world looks like a rainbow exploded. Bengal tigers roam wild in the forests. Indian food can be SO spicy that grown-ups cry. The Taj Mahal is so beautiful people fly across the world just to look at it. India's rail network carries 23 MILLION passengers every single day. That is like moving the entire population of Australia daily. Bollywood produces more films per year than Hollywood.

ISL.letter: Halló! Iceland has 130 volcanoes, and some of them are hiding UNDER glaciers. In summer the sun NEVER goes down, so you could play outside at midnight. Almost everyone believes in elves, and they build tiny elf houses and move ROADS so they don't disturb elf territory. Iceland has no mosquitoes. Zero. Also no army. They have a naming committee that must approve all baby names. And they heat their swimming pools with volcanic water so you can swim outdoors in winter.

CHN.letter: Nǐ hǎo! China built a wall so long you could drive across America FOUR TIMES and still not reach the end. This is the ONLY place on Earth where wild pandas live, and they basically eat bamboo and look cute for a living. China also invented paper, fireworks, and noodles. You're welcome. The Great Wall is so long that walking its entire length would take about 18 MONTHS of non-stop walking. And you can see it from airplanes but NOT from space (that is a myth).

RUS.letter: Privet! Russia is the BIGGEST country in the whole world. It's so big it stretches across 11 time zones. When kids on one side are waking up, kids on the other side are going to bed. Matryoshka dolls have smaller dolls hiding inside. It gets SO cold your eyelashes freeze together. Russia is so wide that it spans 11 time zones. When people in the west are having breakfast, people in the east are already asleep. The Trans-Siberian Railway takes 6 days to cross.

CAN.letter: Hey there, eh! Canada has a MAPLE SYRUP VAULT. Like a bank vault, but full of syrup. In case of emergency. Polar bears just walk through a town called Churchill like they own the place. Kids here learn to skate before they can walk. Canada has 20% of all the world's fresh water. Hudson Bay has so much less gravity than the rest of the planet that you actually weigh slightly less there. Scientists figured out why in 2007.

DEU.letter: Hallo! Germany has a castle that looks EXACTLY like a Disney fairy tale. Neuschwanstein Castle inspired Sleeping Beauty's castle. Pretzels were invented here and kids eat them as big as their HEADS. The teddy bear was named after a German toymaker. Germany has over 1,500 types of beer and over 300 types of bread. The Autobahn has sections with NO SPEED LIMIT. Some people drive 150 mph legally.

ESP.letter: ¡Hola! In Spain, shops close in the afternoon so everyone can take a nap called a 'siesta.' In one town, people throw TOMATOES at each other once a year. On purpose! Flamenco dancers stomp so hard the floor shakes. La Tomatina festival in Buñol uses 150,000 tomatoes in one hour. The streets run RED. Afterward everyone just... hoses off and goes to lunch.

KOR.letter: Annyeonghaseyo! South Korea invented K-pop, and K-pop bands practice dancing for TWELVE HOURS a day (your legs would fall off). South Korea has more robots per person than any country on Earth. They also have the fastest internet in the world, so nobody there has EVER seen a loading screen. South Korea went from one of the poorest countries on Earth to a tech powerhouse in ONE generation. K-pop groups like BTS have more social media followers than most countries have people.

THA.letter: Sawasdee! Thailand loves elephants so much they have a whole DAY to celebrate them. Pad Thai noodles were invented as a national dish and everyone eats them. Thai people greet each other with a little bow called a 'wai.' Try it! Thailand has NEVER been colonized by a European power. It is the only country in Southeast Asia that can say that. Thai food is considered one of the greatest cuisines on Earth.

TUR.letter: Merhaba! Türkiye is the only country that sits on TWO continents at the same time — Europe AND Asia. Cappadocia has rock towers called fairy chimneys and you can fly hot air balloons over them. Turkish delight is a squishy candy that's 500 years old. Istanbul is the only city in the world that sits on TWO continents. The Grand Bazaar has 4,000 shops and has been open since 1461. Turkish breakfasts take up the entire table.

ZAF.letter: Howzit! South Africa has ELEVEN official languages. African penguins live on sunny beaches here — penguins in the SUN! You can see the Big Five all in one place: lion, elephant, buffalo, leopard, and rhino. Table Mountain in Cape Town has a flat top where clouds spill over the edge like a tablecloth. It is one of the oldest mountains on Earth.

ARG.letter: ¡Hola! In Argentina, soccer is almost a RELIGION. Kids play it everywhere, all the time. Argentinians have barbecues called 'asados' that last ALL DAY. Tango was invented here — two people dancing like they're telling a secret. Argentinians eat MORE beef per person than almost anyone on Earth. An asado (barbecue) is an all-day event and the meat never stops coming.

PER.letter: ¡Hola! Peru has fluffy llamas and alpacas living high up in the Andes mountains. Machu Picchu is a secret city in the clouds built 600 years ago. Peru has over 3,000 types of potato. THREE THOUSAND. You thought there was just one kind. Machu Picchu was built so precisely that you cannot fit a credit card between the stones. No mortar was used. The Inca just cut the stones to fit perfectly.

NOR.letter: Hei! Norway is where you can see RAINBOW LIGHTS dancing in the sky — the Northern Lights paint everything green, purple, and pink. There's a hotel made ENTIRELY out of ice. You sleep in an ice bed! Norwegians eat fish for breakfast. Even kids. Norway has SO much coastline (with all the fjords) that if you straightened it out, it would reach halfway around the world. The midnight sun means it never gets dark in summer.

GRC.letter: Yia sou! Greece is where the OLYMPIC GAMES started almost 3,000 years ago. Greek yia-yias (grandmas) put a blue eye charm on babies for good luck. Democracy was invented here. That means 'the people get to choose!' Greece has more archaeological museums than any other country on Earth. The Parthenon has been a temple, a church, a mosque, and an ammunition dump (which exploded in 1687).

NZL.letter: Kia ora! New Zealand is where Lord of the Rings was filmed. The Hobbit's Shire is a REAL place you can visit. People here call themselves 'Kiwis' after their fuzzy national bird. There are SIX sheep for every person! New Zealand gave women the right to vote in 1893, the first country in the world to do so. And they have a town called Taumatawhakatangihangakoauauotamateaturipukakapikimaungahoronukupokaiwhenuakitanatahu. That is the longest place name on Earth.

IDN.letter: Halo! Indonesia is made of over 17,000 islands. SEVENTEEN THOUSAND. Nobody has even visited all of them. Komodo dragons live here — the biggest lizards on Earth, as long as a car. Orangutans swing through the rainforest trees. Kawah Ijen volcano has BLUE FIRE that burns at night from sulfur gas. Workers carry baskets of sulfur up the crater in total darkness.

COL.letter: ¡Hola! Colombia has a river that turns FIVE different colors — red, yellow, green, blue, and black. Most of the world's emeralds come from here. Colombian coffee is so famous they put a coffee farmer on their logo. Caño Cristales (the River of Five Colors) only blooms in certain months when underwater plants turn red, yellow, green, blue, and black all at once.

KAZ.letter: Salam! Kazakhstan is so enormous that it stretches from the edge of Europe to the middle of Asia. The world's first astronaut launch site is here, so SPACE TRAVEL starts in Kazakhstan. Wild horses still run free across the endless grasslands. And the national drink is fermented horse milk. Yes, really. The Baikonur Cosmodrome in Kazakhstan is where almost every astronaut who has gone to space was launched. Almaty means 'full of apples' because the FIRST apples in history grew in this region.

SAU.letter: Marhaba! Saudi Arabia is almost ENTIRELY desert, and some sand dunes are taller than skyscrapers. They're building a city called NEOM that will be just one building, 170 kilometers long, in a straight line. Also, millions of people visit Mecca every year for the largest gathering of humans on Earth. The Empty Quarter (Rub' al Khali) is the largest sand desert on Earth. Dunes reach 800 feet high. Saudi Arabia is building NEOM, a futuristic city with no cars and a straight line of buildings 100 miles long.

ETH.letter: Selam! Ethiopia is where COFFEE was discovered. A goat herder noticed his goats dancing after eating certain berries. Ethiopia also has its own calendar, and right now they're about 7 years behind the rest of the world. Time is literally different here. Ethiopia uses a different CALENDAR. They are about 7 years behind the rest of the world. New Year's is in September. Oh, and the coffee ceremony can take 3 hours because it is a social event, not a caffeine break.

facts.t: Home of the Lion of Judah

MAR.letter: Salaam! Morocco has a city called Chefchaouen where EVERYTHING is painted blue. Every wall, every staircase, every doorway. It looks like someone spilled the sky. The markets (called souks) are mazes of spices, leather, and the smell of fresh mint tea everywhere. The medina in Fez is the largest car-free urban area in the world. Donkeys deliver everything because the alleys are too narrow for vehicles. The tanneries use methods unchanged for 1,000 years.

VNM.letter: Xin chào! Vietnam has MORE motorbikes than cars, and watching traffic is like watching a school of fish. Pho (say 'fuh') is a soup so good that people eat it for BREAKFAST, lunch, AND dinner. Ha Long Bay has thousands of limestone islands sticking out of emerald water like dragon teeth. Halong Bay has thousands of limestone islands rising from emerald water like a painting come to life. The traffic in Ho Chi Minh City is legendary. Thousands of motorbikes flow like water around obstacles.

POL.letter: Cześć! Poland has a legend about a DRAGON that lived under a castle in Kraków. There's still a statue that breathes real fire. Polish people eat pierogi (stuffed dumplings) for every occasion. Happy? Pierogi. Sad? Pierogi. Tuesday? Pierogi. Polish people are among the most educated in Europe. Wroclaw has over 100 tiny bronze gnome statues hidden around the city. Finding them all is a citywide treasure hunt.

UKR.letter: Pryvit! Ukraine is called the 'breadbasket of Europe' because it grows SO much wheat that it helps feed the entire continent. Sunflower fields stretch to the horizon in every direction. Ukrainian Easter eggs (pysanky) are decorated so beautifully that they're considered works of art. Kyiv is older than Moscow. Ukraine's farmland is some of the richest on Earth, with deep black soil that grows wheat for millions of people worldwide.

CUB.letter: ¡Hola! Cuba is like a time machine. The streets are full of classic cars from the 1950s because new cars couldn't come in for decades. Music is EVERYWHERE. People play drums and dance salsa on every corner. The beaches have sand so white it looks like sugar. Old American cars from the 1950s still drive around Havana because Cuba has been cut off from trade for decades. The cars are held together with creativity and determination.

facts.t: More doctors per person than anywhere

CHL.letter: ¡Hola! Chile is the SKINNIEST country on Earth. It's 2,700 miles long but only 110 miles wide. It's like a piece of spaghetti stuck to the side of South America. The Atacama Desert here is the driest place on Earth. Some spots haven't had rain in 400 YEARS. The Atacama Desert is the driest place on Earth. Some weather stations there have NEVER recorded rain. NASA tests Mars equipment here because it is the closest thing to another planet.

PAK.letter: Assalamu alaikum! Pakistan has the SECOND tallest mountain in the world: K2. It's so dangerous that it's harder to climb than Everest. Cricket is basically a religion here, kids play it in every street and alley. And the trucks? They're painted in the wildest, most colorful art you've ever seen. K2 in Pakistan is the world's second tallest mountain but the MOST dangerous to climb. The Karakoram Highway is one of the highest paved roads on Earth, carving through impossible mountain passes.

SWE.letter: Hej! Sweden is where IKEA, Spotify, and Minecraft ALL come from. One tiny country invented your furniture, your music app, AND your favorite game. In winter it's dark for 20 hours a day. In summer the sun barely sets. Swedish kids get 'fika' every day, which is an official snack break. With cake. Sweden is so good at recycling that it ran OUT of garbage and started importing trash from other countries to fuel its waste-to-energy plants. Fika (coffee break) is a sacred daily ritual.

NLD.letter: Hallo! The Netherlands is SO flat that a quarter of the country is BELOW sea level. They literally built walls to hold back the ocean. There are more bicycles than people here. EVERYONE rides bikes, grandmas, businessmen, even the Prime Minister. A quarter of the Netherlands is BELOW sea level. The Dutch literally built their country by pushing the ocean back with dikes and windmills. Without engineering, Amsterdam would be underwater.

PHL.letter: Mabuhay! The Philippines has over 7,000 islands. SEVEN THOUSAND. Even Filipinos haven't visited all of them. Karaoke is basically a national sport here. Every neighborhood has a karaoke machine and nobody is shy. The Philippine Eagle is one of the biggest and rarest birds on Earth. The Philippines has more islands (7,641) than any country you can count in a day. Filipino families are so close that multiple generations often live in one house.

BGD.letter: Assalamu alaikum! Bangladesh has the world's LARGEST river delta, where three massive rivers meet the sea. Bengal tigers swim between islands in the mangrove forests. This tiny country has 170 million people, making it one of the most crowded places on Earth. And the national sport? Cricket, played in every alley.

TZA.letter: Habari! Tanzania has Mount Kilimanjaro, the tallest mountain in ALL of Africa. You can see snow on top even though it's near the equator. The Serengeti has the Great Migration where 2 MILLION animals walk across the plains looking for food. And Zanzibar island smells like cloves and cinnamon. Zanzibar's Stone Town is a maze of narrow streets where the smell of cloves fills the air. The spice trade made this tiny island one of the richest places in East Africa.

DZA.letter: Marhaba! Algeria is the BIGGEST country in all of Africa. Most of it is the Sahara Desert, where sand dunes are taller than buildings. The ancient Roman ruins here are some of the best preserved in the world. Ancient Roman ruins at Timgad sit in the middle of the desert, almost perfectly preserved, like a city frozen in time.

AGO.letter: Olá! Angola has diamonds buried EVERYWHERE underground and waterfalls bigger than Niagara. The music here is called kuduro and it is PHYSICALLY IMPOSSIBLE to hear it without dancing. Your body just moves. Tundavala Gap is a cliff where you stare down 3,000 feet of sheer drop into the clouds. Your stomach flips just standing there.

CMR.letter: Bonjour! Cameroon is called 'Africa in miniature' because it has EVERYTHING: beaches, mountains, rainforests, deserts, and savannas all packed into one country. Mount Cameroon is an active volcano and the highest point in West Africa. The national soccer team is called the Indomitable Lions and they have terrified opponents at multiple World Cups. Cameroon is one of the few countries where both French AND English are official languages. The food is incredible: ndolé (bitterleaf stew) with plantains is the national dish.

COD.letter: Mbote! The Democratic Republic of Congo has the second biggest rainforest on Earth. Mountain gorillas live here, and they share 98% of their DNA with YOU. The Congo River is so deep that no one has found the bottom. The Congo River is so powerful it generates its own weather patterns. In some spots it is 720 feet deep, the deepest river on the planet.

GHA.letter: Ghana produces SO much cocoa that your chocolate bar PROBABLY started right here. When someone dies, families order coffins shaped like airplanes, fish, or Coca-Cola bottles. FANTASY COFFINS. It is the most creative way to say goodbye EVER. Ghanaian fantasy coffins are built to represent the dead person's life. A fisherman gets a fish coffin. A pilot gets an airplane. A cocoa farmer gets a cocoa pod. The funerals are celebrations.

SDN.letter: Marhaba! Sudan has MORE PYRAMIDS THAN EGYPT. Over 200 of them. And almost NOBODY knows they exist. You could visit them and be the ONLY person there. The camel markets here are absolutely wild and the Nile runs right through the middle.'t even know they exist. The Nile River runs right through the middle. Camel markets here are some of the biggest in the world.

SEN.letter: Nanga def! Senegal's national sport is WRESTLING, and matches are treated like the Super Bowl. The capital Dakar is where the famous Paris-Dakar rally ends. Lake Retba is naturally PINK because of bacteria in the water. Lake Retba (the Pink Lake) is so salty that harvesters coat themselves in shea butter to protect their skin while they scoop salt from the bottom.

SOM.letter: Salaam! Somalia has the LONGEST coastline in mainland Africa. Somalis are legendary poets, and poetry competitions are serious business. Camels are so important here that there are dozens of words just for different types of camels. The Laas Geel cave paintings are over 5,000 years old and among the best-preserved in Africa. Nobody knew they existed until 2002.

MOZ.letter: Olá! Mozambique has an AK-47 ON ITS FLAG. The only country in the world with a modern weapon on its flag. Whale sharks, the biggest fish alive, swim right off the beaches. Those beaches are some of the most beautiful in Africa. After years of war, this country is rebuilding beautifully. The island of Mozambique was the capital for 400 years and has buildings from the 1500s. Mozambique's coastline stretches over 1,500 miles with some of the best diving in Africa.

MDG.letter: Salama! Madagascar is an island where 90% of the wildlife exists NOWHERE ELSE on Earth. Lemurs are found only here. Baobab trees look like they were planted upside down. This place is like another planet. The aye-aye is a lemur so weird-looking that some Malagasy people believe seeing one brings bad luck. It taps on trees with a skeleton-like finger to find bugs.

UGA.letter: Uganda is called the Pearl of Africa and once you see it you understand. Half the world's remaining mountain gorillas live here. The source of the ENTIRE Nile River starts at Lake Victoria. And breakfast is called a Rolex, but it's NOT the watch. It's a chapati rolled around an omelet. The source of the Nile River is in Uganda. The Ugandan Rolex is not a watch. It is a chapati wrapped around an omelet sold on every street corner. It is the best street food name ever.

ZMB.letter: Zambia has Victoria Falls, where water crashes down so hard the spray shoots THIRTY MILES into the sky. Locals call it the smoke that thunders. Zambians are legendarily friendly and somehow crammed 19 national parks into one country. Walking safaris were basically invented here. Instead of driving past animals, you walk among them with a guide. It is as terrifying and exhilarating as it sounds.

ZWE.letter: Zimbabwe has ancient stone ruins built WITHOUT mortar, cement, or glue. Just perfectly stacked rocks standing for 900 years. The stone bird from these ruins is so iconic it's on the flag AND the money. Victoria Falls thunders on the border. Great Zimbabwe was a medieval city of stone towers built without mortar. Europeans refused to believe Africans built it. They were wrong. The stone bird from the ruins is on the flag.

NER.letter: Bonjour! Niger has DINOSAUR FOSSILS just lying around in the Sahara Desert. The Tuareg people wear so much indigo that their SKIN turns blue. The Tuareg people are called 'blue people' because their indigo clothes dye their skin. The Gerewol festival is a male beauty contest where Wodaabe men spend HOURS applying makeup, rolling their eyes, and showing their teeth to impress women judges.

MLI.letter: I ni ce! While Europe was stumbling around in the Dark Ages, Timbuktu in Mali had UNIVERSITIES with hundreds of thousands of books. Mali was basically the Harvard of the ancient world and nobody teaches you that. The Great Mosque of Djenné is the largest mud-brick building in the world. Every year the whole town replasters it by hand, together, in one day.

TCD.letter: Salaam! Chad has ancient cave paintings THOUSANDS of years old just sitting in the desert. Lake Chad used to be enormous but the Sahara is literally EATING the country from the north. It is disappearing. The Ennedi Plateau has natural rock arches that rival anything in Utah, and almost no tourists have ever seen them.

IRQ.letter: Iraq is where CIVILIZATION STARTED. Writing, the wheel, mathematics, the concept of 60 minutes in an hour, ALL invented right here between the Tigris and Euphrates rivers. The ancient city of Babylon was here. This land is literally where human history began. The Hanging Gardens of Babylon, one of the Seven Wonders of the Ancient World, were supposedly here. The marshes of southern Iraq are home to the Ma'dan people who build floating houses entirely from reeds.

ISR.letter: Israel is TINY but has more tech startups per person than almost anywhere on Earth. The Dead Sea is so salty you literally CANNOT sink. You float whether you want to or not. This small piece of land has been argued over for thousands of years. Jerusalem is holy to THREE religions at the same time: Judaism, Christianity, and Islam. That is why it is the most contested piece of land on Earth.

JOR.letter: Jordan has Petra, an entire city CARVED into pink rock cliffs. Indiana Jones was filmed here. The Dead Sea border is the lowest point on Earth. Jordanian hospitality is so legendary that refusing food is basically impossible. The Dead Sea is so salty you FLOAT without trying. You literally cannot sink. People read newspapers while floating on their backs.

SYR.letter: Syria has Damascus, where people have lived for ELEVEN THOUSAND YEARS straight. Older than the pyramids. Older than Stonehenge. Older than basically everything. They also invented SOAP here. The entire world's hygiene started in Syria. Aleppo soap, still made the same way for 4,000 years, is considered one of the finest soaps on Earth. Aleppo was one of the oldest continuously inhabited cities before the war.

YEM.letter: Yemen built SKYSCRAPERS out of MUD. Centuries ago. And they're STILL standing. Socotra island has trees that look like alien plants. The word mocha comes from a Yemeni port because the entire world's coffee obsession started HERE. The Socotra dragon blood tree looks like a flying saucer landed and grew roots. The red sap was used as dye and medicine for thousands of years.

OMN.letter: Oman's ancient sailors reached CHINA over 1,000 years ago in wooden boats. Frankincense trees still grow here, the same stuff from the Christmas story. The wadis are stunning turquoise pools hidden in the desert mountains. The Sultan Qaboos Grand Mosque has a hand-loomed carpet that weighs 21 TONS and took 600 women four years to make.

ARE.letter: The UAE went from empty desert to the world's MOST FUTURISTIC cities in just 50 years. Dubai has the tallest building on Earth AND an indoor ski slope. IN THE DESERT. They built islands shaped like PALM TREES visible from space. The Dubai Mall has an actual AQUARIUM inside it with sharks. You can go shopping and watch sharks swim by. The world's tallest building is literally next door.

QAT.letter: Qatar is one of the richest countries on Earth per person and it hosted the 2022 World Cup. This tiny peninsula in the Persian Gulf built some of the most futuristic architecture ANYWHERE. The air conditioning bills alone could fund a small country. Qatar is so rich from natural gas that citizens pay NO income tax and the government provides free healthcare and education. The 2022 World Cup was held here in air-conditioned stadiums.

facts.t: One of the richest per person

KWT.letter: Kuwait is so rich from oil that citizens pay ZERO income tax. The government basically pays YOU to live here. In 2012 they set the world record for the biggest fireworks display. Traditional wooden boats still sail alongside gleaming skyscrapers. Kuwait's Towers are the national symbol and they look like something from a science fiction movie. Summer temperatures regularly hit 130°F, making it one of the hottest places humans live.

LBN.letter: Lebanon's food is so good that EVERY country tries to copy it. Hummus, shawarma, baklava, ALL invented here. The ancient Phoenicians invented THE ALPHABET. The actual letters you are reading RIGHT NOW evolved from Lebanese writing. Despite everything, Beirut rebuilt itself after civil war and is famous for its nightlife. The Lebanese can switch between Arabic, French, and English in a single sentence.

AFG.letter: Afghanistan is where kite fighting is a SERIOUS sport. Kids battle kites with glass-coated strings trying to cut each other down. The Hindu Kush mountains are jaw-dropping. Afghan carpets are so beautiful they hang in museums. The Bamiyan Valley once had two enormous Buddha statues carved into cliffs 1,500 years ago. The Taliban destroyed them, but the empty spaces remain.

MMR.letter: Mingalaba! Bagan in Myanmar has OVER 2,000 ancient temples crammed into one area. Hot air balloons float between them at sunrise and it looks FAKE but it is completely real. The rubies here are so rare that one can cost more than a house. Bagan has over 2,000 ancient temples spread across a plain. At sunrise, hot air balloons float over them. It rivals Angkor Wat but with a fraction of the tourists.

NPL.letter: Nepal has Mount Everest, the TALLEST mountain on Earth. Sherpa climbers are the bravest mountaineers alive. Nepal's flag is the ONLY national flag that is not a rectangle. It's two triangles stacked up. Every other country: rectangle. Nepal: nope. Sherpas who guide climbers up Everest are some of the toughest athletes on Earth. Nepal's flag is the only national flag that is NOT a rectangle. It is two stacked triangles.

LKA.letter: Sri Lanka is shaped like a teardrop falling off India. Elephants casually walk through ancient Buddhist temples like they own the place. The cinnamon in your kitchen? Sri Lanka was the ORIGINAL source. The tea here is so good the British fought WARS over it. The ancient city of Sigiriya is a fortress built on top of a massive rock 660 feet high. The king who built it was so paranoid about his brother that he put his palace where nobody could reach him.

MYS.letter: Malaysia has a rainforest that is 130 MILLION years old. Older than the DINOSAURS. The food is insane because Malay, Chinese, and Indian cooking all smashed together. Nine sultans take TURNS being king. They rotate the throne every 5 years. The Petronas Towers have a sky bridge between them at the 41st floor. Malaysian food is SO good that people fly to Penang just to eat. Nasi lemak at 3 AM is a national tradition.

MNG.letter: Mongolia has MORE HORSES THAN PEOPLE. Families still live in round tents called gers and herd animals across the world's biggest grassland. Genghis Khan built the LARGEST land empire in human history from right here. Eagle hunters still catch prey with trained golden eagles. Mongolia has the world's coldest capital city (Ulaanbaatar). Nomadic families still move their entire ger (yurt) home several times a year following their herds across the steppe.

KHM.letter: Cambodia has Angkor Wat, the LARGEST religious building EVER constructed. It is so massive it can be seen from space. The Khmer alphabet has 74 letters, more than any other alphabet on Earth. Despite terrible recent history, Cambodians are incredibly resilient. The Khmer Rouge regime destroyed nearly everything in the 1970s. Cambodia rebuilt from almost nothing. Today, Angkor Wat draws millions, and the street food in Phnom Penh is incredible.

LAO.letter: Laos is the Land of a Million Elephants and every morning THOUSANDS of monks in orange robes walk through the streets collecting food. The most peaceful thing you have ever seen. Also the most bombed country per person in history. And somehow it remains gentle. Every morning in Luang Prabang, hundreds of monks walk through town in orange robes collecting food from residents. The tradition has continued for centuries. It is one of the most beautiful sights in Asia.

UZB.letter: Uzbekistan has Samarkand, covered in turquoise tiles so beautiful your jaw literally drops. The Silk Road ran right through here. Uzbek pilaf (plov) is so sacred that master chefs cook it in pots the SIZE OF BATHTUBS. At Uzbek weddings, the plov is cooked in a single pot big enough to feed 1,000 people. The plov master is one of the most respected people in the community.

TJK.letter: Salom! Tajikistan is NINETY-THREE PERCENT mountains. Almost the entire country is just peaks, glaciers, and valleys so deep the sun only reaches the bottom for a few hours a day. The Pamir Highway winds along cliffs so high your ears pop, and it is considered one of the most dangerous and most beautiful roads on Earth. People here drink green tea all day long. The Fann Mountains have turquoise lakes that look like someone poured paint into them. Despite being one of the poorest countries in Central Asia, the hospitality is legendary.

TKM.letter: Salam! Turkmenistan has a giant crater that has been on fire for over 50 YEARS. In 1971, scientists drilling for gas accidentally created a hole that collapsed. They lit it on fire thinking it would burn out in a few weeks. It never stopped. They call it the 'Door to Hell.' The Akhal-Teke horses from here are so shiny and golden that they look like they are made of metal. Turkmenistan is one of the most isolated countries on Earth. The capital Ashgabat is made almost entirely of white marble and holds a Guinness World Record for it.

SGP.letter: Singapore is a CITY that is also a COUNTRY. One of the smallest nations on Earth but one of the richest. Chewing gum is BANNED. Gardens by the Bay has giant mechanical trees. The food courts serve the best cheap food in the world. At the Night Safari, you ride through a zoo in total darkness watching animals who are awake at night. Also, jaywalking is a crime and the streets are so clean you could eat off them.

TWN.letter: Taiwan makes the computer chips inside your phone, your laptop, your EVERYTHING. Without this one small island the whole tech world would collapse. They invented BUBBLE TEA. The night markets sell stinky tofu that smells terrible but tastes incredible. The night markets in Taiwan are LEGENDARY. Entire streets transform into food paradises after dark. Stinky tofu smells terrible but tastes amazing. Taipei 101 was the world's tallest building until 2010.

PRK.letter: North Korea is so isolated that almost nobody from the outside world has EVER been allowed in. At night, satellite photos show South Korea lit up like a Christmas tree and North Korea almost completely DARK. The same family has ruled since 1948. North Korea has its own time zone, its own internet (with only about 28 websites), and its own calendar that starts from the birth year of Kim Il-sung.

PRT.letter: Portugal's sailors explored more of the world than almost anyone in history. They reached Brazil, India, Africa, and Japan FIRST. Pasteis de nata custard tarts are so good people fly to Lisbon JUST to eat them. Portugal produces half the world's CORK. Portuguese custard tarts (pastéis de nata) have a SECRET recipe kept by monks for centuries. Lisbon's trams are so old and charming they are a tourist attraction by themselves.

AUT.letter: Austria is where Mozart, the most famous musician EVER, was born. The Sound of Music was filmed in the Alps here. Viennese coffee houses have been serving cake and arguing about art for 300 YEARS. And they invented Red Bull. Vienna has been ranked the most livable city on Earth for years running. And the Viennese will argue about which coffeehouse has the best Sachertorte until the sun goes down.

CHE.letter: Switzerland makes the world's best chocolate AND the world's best watches. James Bond has been filmed here multiple times. They haven't been in a WAR for over 500 years. And CERN, where the World Wide Web was invented, is here. The Swiss rail system is so precise that if a train is 3 minutes late, the railway issues a formal apology. The country also has enough nuclear bunkers to shelter EVERY citizen.

BEL.letter: Belgium makes the finest chocolate on Earth AND invented Belgian waffles AND invented French fries. Yes, FRENCH fries are actually BELGIAN. This tiny country punches way above its weight in deliciousness. The Manneken Pis is a famous statue of a little boy peeing into a fountain. Brussels loves it so much they dress it in different costumes hundreds of times a year.

DNK.letter: Denmark is one of the HAPPIEST countries on Earth. LEGO was invented here. The Little Mermaid statue sits in Copenhagen harbor. Danish people practice hygge, which means making everything cozy and warm. And they have FREE healthcare. Danes leave their babies sleeping in strollers OUTSIDE restaurants and shops, even in winter. It is totally normal here and considered healthy for the baby.

FIN.letter: Finland is where Santa Claus OFFICIALLY lives. In Lapland. Near the Arctic Circle. Finns have more SAUNAS than cars. The Northern Lights are visible here. They drink more COFFEE per person than anyone. And their education system is the best in the world. Finland has more saunas than cars. Almost every house has one. Finns sit in 200°F heat and then jump into frozen lakes. On purpose. They say it makes you feel alive.

CZE.letter: Prague is one of the most beautiful cities in Europe with a castle so big it's in the Guinness Book of Records. Czechs drink more BEER per person than ANY other country on Earth. They invented contact lenses AND the word ROBOT. Prague's Astronomical Clock has been ticking since 1410. Every hour, mechanical skeletons and apostles perform. Tourists gather just to watch a 600-year-old clock.

HUN.letter: Hungary invented the RUBIK'S CUBE and it has driven people insane for decades. Budapest has thermal baths where people have soaked for 2,000 YEARS. Goulash was invented by Hungarian COWBOYS. Not chefs. Cowboys. Lake Balaton is called the Hungarian Sea because the country is landlocked and this is the closest thing to a beach. Hungarians LOVE it.

ROU.letter: Romania has Transylvania, home of DRACULA. Bran Castle is the real Dracula's Castle and yes you can visit it. The Carpathian Mountains have Europe's last truly WILD forests with bears and wolves roaming free. Medieval Europe is alive here. Transylvania is a REAL place, not just a Dracula story. The Carpathian Mountains have brown bears, wolves, and lynx. Romania also has the happiest cemetery in the world: Merry Cemetery, with colorful tombstones telling funny stories.

SRB.letter: Serbia is where Nikola Tesla was born, the genius who invented the electricity that powers YOUR house. Serbians celebrate TWO birthdays and TWO New Years every year. Belgrade is one of the oldest continuously inhabited cities in Europe. Nikola Tesla, the genius who invented AC electricity (the type that powers your house), was Serbian. Without Tesla, you would not have electricity the way you use it today.

HRV.letter: Croatia's coastline is so stunning that GAME OF THRONES was filmed in Dubrovnik. There are over 1,000 islands. And the NECKTIE was invented here. The word cravat comes from Croat. Every business meeting in the world owes Croatia a thank you. Dubrovnik's old city walls were used as King's Landing in Game of Thrones. The city is so beautiful that tourists are overwhelming it. Croatia has over 1,000 islands.

BGR.letter: In Bulgaria, shaking your head means YES and nodding means NO. The OPPOSITE of everyone else on Earth. Imagine the confusion. The Valley of Roses grows more roses than anywhere. The Cyrillic alphabet you see in Russian? Actually created HERE. The Valley of Roses produces most of the world's rose oil. It takes 3,000 kilograms of rose petals to make ONE kilogram of oil. Bulgarian yogurt has bacteria found nowhere else on Earth.

SVK.letter: Ahoj! Slovakia has MORE CASTLES PER PERSON than anywhere on Earth. Over 180 of them. Brown bears just casually wander through the mountains. And Bratislava is the only capital city that touches TWO other countries at once. Bratislava is the only capital city in the world that borders TWO other countries (Austria and Hungary). You can walk to another country in 15 minutes.

SVN.letter: Živjo! Slovenia is one of the greenest countries in the world. Over half the country is covered in forest. Ljubljana, the capital, has a dragon on its bridge. The Postojna Cave has baby dragons (actually blind salamanders called olms). Lake Bled has a tiny island in the middle with a church. Tradition says the groom must carry the bride up 99 steps to the church for good luck.

EST.letter: Tere! Estonia is so digital you can vote, pay taxes, and start a business from your PHONE. They invented Skype. The medieval old town looks like a literal fairy tale, and there are more saunas than people.'s straight out of a fairy tale. Estonia was the first country to hold elections where people could vote ONLINE from their couch. Estonians can do almost everything government-related on their phones.

LTU.letter: Labas! Lithuania is OBSESSED with basketball. It's basically the national religion. The Hill of Crosses has over 200,000 crosses placed by visitors. And Lithuanian is one of the oldest living languages in the world. During Soviet rule, Lithuanians secretly published banned books and smuggled them across borders. The book smugglers are national heroes.

LUX.letter: Moien! Luxembourg is one of the smallest AND richest countries in the world. It has more banks per person than almost anywhere. The old town sits on dramatic cliffs with fortifications from the Middle Ages. People who live here earn more per person than almost anywhere. Luxembourg is smaller than Rhode Island but richer than almost every country on Earth.

CYP.letter: Yia sou! Cyprus is an island where you can ski in the morning and swim in the afternoon. The goddess Aphrodite was born here according to Greek mythology. Halloumi cheese was invented here and it's the only cheese you can GRILL. The island has been divided by a UN buffer zone since 1974. There is literally a line through the capital city Nicosia where one side is Greek and the other is Turkish.

ALB.letter: Albania was so isolated for decades that when it finally opened up tourists discovered STUNNING beaches nobody knew about. 700,000 concrete bunkers dot the landscape from the old paranoid government. The country is called the Land of Eagles. During communist rule, Albania built 700,000 concrete bunkers. They are EVERYWHERE. On beaches, in fields, on mountains. Now people turn them into cafes and art installations.

GEO.letter: Georgia claims to be the birthplace of WINE. They have been making it for 8,000 YEARS in clay pots buried underground. Georgian hospitality means they will feed you until you BEG them to stop. The Caucasus Mountains are dramatic. A traditional Georgian feast (supra) has a toastmaster who leads dozens of elaborate toasts. Refusing a toast is basically a crime. You WILL drink more than you planned.

VEN.letter: Venezuela has Angel Falls, the TALLEST waterfall in the world. The water drops so far it turns to MIST before hitting the bottom. Venezuela also has more lightning storms than anywhere on Earth. And more Miss Universe winners than any other country. Angel Falls is the tallest waterfall on Earth, dropping 3,212 feet. The water falls so far that much of it turns to mist before reaching the bottom.

ECU.letter: Ecuador is literally named after the EQUATOR which runs right through it. You can stand with one foot in each hemisphere. The Galapagos Islands are here, where Darwin's giant tortoises still roam. And panama hats? Actually made in ECUADOR. Not Panama. Standing on the equator here, you can put one foot in the Northern Hemisphere and one in the Southern. Ecuador also has the closest point on Earth's surface to the sun (Mount Chimborazo, not Everest).

BOL.letter: Bolivia has the world's largest salt flat, so perfectly white and flat it looks like you're walking on a MIRROR. La Paz is the highest capital city on Earth. And Bolivia has TWO capital cities, not one. Because why not. During the rainy season, the salt flat fills with a thin layer of water and becomes the world's largest mirror. You literally walk on the sky.

PRY.letter: ¡Hola! Paraguay is the quietest country in South America. Almost everyone speaks two languages: Spanish AND Guaraní (an indigenous language). The Itaipú Dam produces more electricity than almost any other dam on Earth. Paraguay's Itaipu Dam was once the biggest in the world, providing almost ALL the country's electricity from falling water alone.

URY.letter: Uruguay hosted the very FIRST FIFA World Cup in 1930 and WON IT. This tiny country has more COWS than people. Uruguay is one of the most progressive countries in South America and consistently rated one of the safest. José Mujica, a former president, donated 90% of his salary to charity and drove an old Volkswagen Beetle to work. He was called the world's poorest president.

GUY.letter: Hello! Guyana has a waterfall FOUR TIMES taller than Niagara Falls and almost nobody on Earth knows it exists. 80% of the country is untouched rainforest. It is the only country in South America where everyone speaks English. Kaieteur Falls is five times taller than Niagara and almost nobody visits because it is deep in the rainforest. Tiny golden frogs live in the giant bromeliads at the top.

SUR.letter: Hallo! Suriname is the smallest country in South America and 94% is covered in rainforest. It's the only country in South America where DUTCH is the official language. The food here mixes Indian, Javanese, Chinese, and Creole flavors. The central market in Paramaribo has a section for every cuisine: Javanese, Indian, Creole, Chinese. You can eat your way around Asia and the Caribbean in one building.

GTM.letter: Guatemala has Maya pyramids that stick up ABOVE the jungle canopy like something from a movie. The Maya invented chocolate DRINKS here thousands of years ago. The national bird is so beautiful it's on the money AND it's the NAME of the money. Tikal's temples rise above the jungle canopy so dramatically that they were used as the rebel base in the original Star Wars. Howler monkeys scream from the treetops.

HND.letter: Honduras has the SECOND biggest coral reef in the world and the scuba diving is so cheap that divers come from everywhere. The ancient Maya city of Copan has stone carvings so detailed they look like they were done YESTERDAY. The Bay Islands have some of the cheapest scuba diving certification in the entire world, which is why divers flock here from everywhere.

NIC.letter: ¡Hola! Nicaragua has the only freshwater lake in the world with SHARKS. Bull sharks swim up the San Juan River into Lake Nicaragua. You can sandboard down an active volcano here. It's the biggest country in Central America. You can SANDBOARD down Cerro Negro, an active volcano. You hike up with a board and slide down on volcanic gravel at up to 50 miles per hour.

SLV.letter: ¡Hola! El Salvador is the smallest country in Central America but has BIG personality. It was the first country to make Bitcoin legal money. The surfing here is world-class. Pupusas are the national food and they're incredible. The surfing beaches on the Pacific coast get perfect waves that attract surfers from around the world. And pupusas cost about 50 cents each.

CRI.letter: Pura Vida means pure life and Costa Ricans say it ALL the time. This country has NO ARMY. They spent the military budget on schools and parks instead. 25% is protected nature reserves full of sloths, monkeys, and toucans. Costa Rica generates almost ALL its electricity from renewable sources: water, wind, and geothermal. And sloths are everywhere. EVERYWHERE.

PAN.letter: Panama has a CANAL that lets ships sail between the Atlantic and Pacific oceans without going around South America. It took 10 years and 75,000 workers. And Panama hats? NOT from Panama. They're from Ecuador. Panama is just better at marketing. Ships pay up to $1 MILLION to pass through the Panama Canal. The canal uses a system of locks that LIFT ships up and over the mountains. Engineering genius.

JAM.letter: Jamaica gave the world reggae music, Bob Marley, jerk chicken, and the FASTEST HUMAN ALIVE. Usain Bolt ran faster than anyone in HISTORY. This small island has produced an absolutely ridiculous number of world-class sprinters. The Blue Mountains grow some of the most expensive coffee on Earth. Jerk chicken is slow-smoked over pimento wood with scotch bonnet peppers so hot they make your eyes water from across the room. Jamaica's motto is 'Out of Many, One People' because the island is a mix of African, European, Indian, and Chinese heritage.

DOM.letter: The Dominican Republic produces MORE Major League Baseball players than any country outside America. The talent pipeline runs through here. Merengue music makes your feet move before your brain gives permission. The island was the first place Columbus landed in the Americas. The oldest cathedral, oldest hospital, and oldest university in the New World are all here.

HTI.letter: Haiti was the FIRST country of enslaved people to win independence, in 1804. The Citadelle Laferriere is the largest fortress in the Americas. Haitian art is famous worldwide for vivid, explosive colors that jump off the canvas. Haitian Vodou is a real religion practiced by millions, not the Hollywood version with dolls and curses. The art and music that come from it are extraordinary.

TTO.letter: Trinidad and Tobago invented the STEEL DRUM, the only new acoustic instrument created in the entire 20th century. Carnival here rivals Brazil's. And the national dish involves a SHARK in a sandwich. Yes. Shark. In bread. The Pitch Lake is the largest natural deposit of asphalt on Earth. You can literally walk on it. It supplied the asphalt for roads all over the world.

PNG.letter: Papua New Guinea has over 800 LANGUAGES. EIGHT HUNDRED. More than any country on Earth. Some tribes in the highlands have barely had contact with the outside world. Birds of paradise with stunning feathers live nowhere else. The Asaro Mudmen tribe covers their bodies in white mud and wears terrifying masks to scare enemies. It started when they accidentally scared a rival tribe while covered in river mud.

DJI.letter: Djibouti is one of the HOTTEST countries on Earth. Lake Assal is saltier than the Dead Sea and the lowest point in all of Africa. The landscape looks like the surface of Mars. Whale sharks swim right off the coast. The landscape is so alien that scientists use it to study what Mars might look like. Flamingos somehow live here alongside the volcanic rock.

ERI.letter: Eritrea has a historic railway that winds through DRAMATIC mountain passes, built by the Italians. The capital Asmara has more Art Deco buildings than anywhere outside of Miami. The Dahlak Islands have untouched coral reefs. Asmara's Art Deco and Modernist buildings are so well preserved that the entire city center is a UNESCO World Heritage Site. It looks frozen in the 1930s.

GAB.letter: Bonjour! Gabon is 88% covered in rainforest and has more forest elephants, gorillas, and chimpanzees per square mile than almost anywhere. The beaches here have hippos AND whales, sometimes at the same time. In Loango National Park, you can see hippos surfing ocean waves. HIPPOS. In the OCEAN. The president was overthrown by his own military in 2023 after the Bongo family ruled for 55 years straight.

COG.letter: Mbote! The Republic of Congo (not to be confused with DR Congo next door) has one of the last great rainforests on Earth. Gorillas, forest elephants, and chimps live here. Brazzaville is the only capital directly across a river from another capital (Kinshasa). Brazzaville and Kinshasa are the two closest capital cities in the world. You can literally see one country's capital from the other, separated only by the Congo River.

CAF.letter: The Central African Republic sits right in the HEART of Africa. Diamonds hide in the ground. Forest elephants walk through rainforests most humans have never entered. It is one of the least visited countries on Earth. Despite the instability, the Dzanga forest clearing is one of the last places on Earth where you can watch 50+ forest elephants gathering at once.

SSD.letter: Hello! South Sudan is the NEWEST country on Earth, born in 2011. It's younger than you! The Sudd is one of the biggest wetlands in the world. Despite challenges, the people celebrated independence with incredible joy. The world's second-largest animal migration (after the Serengeti) happens here, with millions of antelope crossing the plains.

RWA.letter: Rwanda is the CLEANEST country in Africa. Plastic bags are completely banned. Once a month everyone stops everything and cleans together. After unimaginable tragedy, Rwanda rebuilt into something extraordinary. Mountain gorillas live in misty volcanoes. Kigali is considered one of the cleanest cities in Africa. The last Saturday of every month, the entire country stops what it is doing for community cleaning. Everyone. Including the president.

MWI.letter: Malawi is called the Warm Heart of Africa because the people are SO friendly it almost feels suspicious. Lake Malawi has more fish species than any other lake on Earth. Over a THOUSAND types. Scientists keep finding new ones. Lake Malawi is so big it looks like an ocean. Fishermen go out in wooden boats at night with lanterns, and the lake lights up like a city.

facts.t: Most fish species in a lake

SLE.letter: Sierra Leone means Lion Mountain. Some of the world's LARGEST diamonds have been found here, including one the size of a small egg. The beaches are stunning. After civil war and Ebola, Sierra Leoneans rebuilt with incredible spirit. Bunce Island was a major slave trading fortress. Today it is an important memorial. The connection between Sierra Leone and the Gullah Geechee people in South Carolina is direct and documented.

LBR.letter: Liberia was founded by freed American slaves and the flag looks ALMOST exactly like the American flag. The name literally means Land of the Free. Africa's first female head of state was elected HERE in 2006. The capital Monrovia is named after US President James Monroe. It is one of the only non-American cities named after a US president.

GIN.letter: Guinea has the world's LARGEST reserves of bauxite, the rock that becomes the aluminum in your soda can. The djembe drum was born here and now every music class in every school in the world has one. You're welcome. Guinea's Fouta Djallon highlands are the source of major West African rivers, including the Niger, Senegal, and Gambia. Three countries depend on water that starts here.

TGO.letter: Togo is one of Africa's smallest countries, shaped like a thin strip. Voodoo is a real, practiced religion here, not the movie version. The tata somba tower houses are so unique they look like they grew out of the ground. The fetish market in Lomé sells dried animal parts, powders, and charms used in traditional medicine. It is the largest voodoo market in the world.

BEN.letter: Bonjour! Benin had an ancient kingdom with an ALL-FEMALE WARRIOR ARMY called the Dahomey Amazons. European soldiers were TERRIFIED of them. Voodoo religion was born right here. And there is an entire village built on STILTS in a lake. The Dahomey Amazons were so fierce that when France tried to colonize, the female warriors were the LAST soldiers still fighting. The movie 'The Woman King' is based on them.

GNQ.letter: ¡Hola! Equatorial Guinea is the only Spanish-speaking country in Africa. Oil made it one of the richest per person in Africa (though the wealth isn't evenly shared). Bioko Island has dramatic volcanic peaks covered in jungle. Bioko Island has one of the highest densities of primates in Africa, and some beaches are nesting sites for endangered sea turtles.

NAM.letter: Namibia has the OLDEST desert on Earth, 5 million years old, with orange sand dunes that glow at sunset. The night sky is SO dark and clear that you can see the Milky Way with your bare eyes. Desert elephants somehow survive in the sand. The Skeleton Coast is littered with shipwrecks and whale bones in the fog. Desert elephants survive here despite almost no water. The Namib Desert is the oldest desert on Earth.

BWA.letter: Dumela! Botswana found DIAMONDS and went from one of the poorest countries on Earth to one of the richest in Africa in a single generation. It has more elephants than any other country. Every year the Okavango Delta floods and turns desert into paradise. Botswana went from one of the poorest countries on Earth to one of Africa's success stories by investing diamond money in education and infrastructure instead of stealing it.

SWZ.letter: Sawubona! Eswatini (formerly Swaziland) is Africa's last absolute monarchy. The king can have multiple wives. The Reed Dance is an annual ceremony where thousands of young women dance. Despite being tiny, the landscapes are beautiful. The king changes the country's name from Swaziland to Eswatini in 2018 on the 50th anniversary of independence. He just... announced it. And that was that.

LSO.letter: Lumela! Lesotho is the only country in the world where EVERY single point of land is above 1,000 meters. It is completely surrounded by South Africa, like a mountain kingdom floating inside another country. Basotho people wear beautiful woven blankets as traditional clothing, even in summer. The blankets are so iconic that they appear on the national flag. Lesotho has a ski resort, which surprises people who do not think of Africa as having snow. The Basotho pony is tough enough to handle the steep mountain trails where no car can go.

MRT.letter: Salaam! Mauritania has one of the longest trains in the world, sometimes stretching 2 KILOMETERS with iron ore. The Eye of the Sahara (Richat Structure) is a mysterious circle visible from space that scientists still debate. The iron ore train is sometimes the longest in the world (over 2 km) and passengers can ride on top of the ore cars for free. It is the only transportation across the desert for some communities.

GMB.letter: Peace! Gambia is the smallest country in mainland Africa. It's basically just the banks of the Gambia River. Despite its tiny size, it has beautiful beaches and incredible birdwatching. It's completely surrounded by Senegal except for the coastline. The Gambia is shaped like a snake: 300 miles long but only 20 miles wide, completely surrounded by Senegal except for its Atlantic coastline. It is one of Africa's smallest countries.

GNB.letter: Guinea-Bissau has the Bijagos Islands where SALTWATER HIPPOS swim in the actual ocean. Hippos. In the SEA. This tiny country is also one of the world's biggest cashew exporters. Almost every cashew you eat might have started here. The Bijagos archipelago is so remote that some islands have never had permanent human residents. Saltwater hippos patrol the beaches like security guards.

TUN.letter: Marhaba! Tunisia has the ruins of ancient Carthage, which once rivaled Rome as the most powerful city in the world. Star Wars was filmed in the Saharan south. In 2011, Tunisia started the Arab Spring by standing up for democracy. The town of Sidi Bou Said is painted entirely in blue and white and looks like it belongs in a painting. George Lucas named the planet Tatooine after the Tunisian town Tataouine.

LBY.letter: Marhaba! Libya is 90% Sahara Desert but has stunning Roman ruins on the Mediterranean coast. Leptis Magna is one of the best-preserved Roman cities ANYWHERE. Libya has huge oil reserves that made it one of Africa's richest countries. The Ubari Sand Sea has brilliant turquoise lakes surrounded by enormous sand dunes. Some are salty, some are fresh, and they look completely impossible in the middle of the Sahara.

NCL.letter: Bonjour from the other side of the world! New Caledonia is a French territory in the Pacific with the world's largest lagoon. The coral reef here is second only to Australia's Great Barrier Reef. Unique pine trees shaped like columns grow NOWHERE else on Earth. New Caledonia held THREE votes on whether to leave France (2018, 2020, 2021) and the answer was complicated every time. The indigenous Kanak people have been fighting for independence for decades. Meanwhile, the island sits on about 25% of the world's known nickel reserves, which makes it strategically important to everyone.

PRI.letter: ¡Hola! Puerto Rico is a US territory with its own incredible culture, and the relationship is complicated. Puerto Ricans are US citizens but cannot vote for president. The island has voted to become a state multiple times but Congress has not acted on it. Reggaetón music was basically invented here (Bad Bunny, Daddy Yankee, all of it). The bioluminescent bays glow neon blue at night when you disturb the water. Old San Juan has colorful colonial streets older than any city in the mainland US. The food is legendary: mofongo (mashed fried plantains with garlic) is the dish everyone argues about whose grandmother makes it best.

BFA.letter: Barka! Burkina Faso means 'Land of Honest People.' It hosts Africa's biggest film festival (FESPACO). The country is known for its incredible music and art scene despite being one of the poorest countries on Earth. Thomas Sankara, called Africa's Che Guevara, tried to make Burkina Faso self-sufficient in just four years before he was assassinated. He is still a hero across the continent.

CIV.letter: Bonjour! Ivory Coast (Côte d'Ivoire) is the world's BIGGEST cocoa producer. Almost HALF of the world's chocolate starts right here. That chocolate bar you ate? There is a very good chance it began as a cocoa pod on an Ivorian farm. Abidjan is one of the most modern cities in West Africa, with skyscrapers, French restaurants, and traffic jams. The country has over 60 ethnic groups and the music scene (coupé-décalé and zouglou) makes everyone dance. The Basilica of Yamoussoukro is modeled after St. Peter's in Rome and is one of the largest churches on Earth.

ARM.letter: Barev! Armenia was the first country in history to adopt Christianity as its national religion, way back in 301 AD. Mount Ararat, where Noah's Ark supposedly landed, is visible from the capital even though it's technically in Turkey. Armenians are SO passionate about chess that it is a mandatory school subject. Every child learns chess. The country has produced world champions despite having only 3 million people.

AZE.letter: Salam! Azerbaijan is called the 'Land of Fire' because natural gas seeps through the ground and creates flames that have been burning for THOUSANDS of years. Baku, the capital, has some of the most futuristic buildings on Earth. The Flame Towers in Baku are covered in LED screens and at night they display enormous flickering flames visible from across the city. The whole country leans into the fire theme.

BLR.letter: Pryvitannie! Belarus has Europe's largest remaining primeval forest, Białowieża, where European bison (wisent) still roam wild. These massive animals were hunted to extinction in the wild by 1927, but Belarus brought them back from just 54 animals in zoos. Now thousands roam the forest again. Belarus also has a surprisingly strong tech scene: the app Viber and the game World of Tanks were both created here. The president has been in power since 1994, making Belarus one of the most unusual governments in Europe.

BIH.letter: Zdravo! Bosnia has the Stari Most, a beautiful Ottoman bridge in Mostar that people dive off for sport. The bridge was destroyed during the 1990s war and the whole world helped rebuild it. Bosnian coffee culture is so serious that a cup takes 30 minutes to prepare and drink properly, served in a special copper pot called a džezva. Sarajevo hosted the 1984 Winter Olympics. The country has three presidents at the same time (one Bosniak, one Croat, one Serb), rotating who is in charge. It is one of the most complex political systems on Earth.

BDI.letter: Amahoro! Burundi is one of the smallest countries in Africa but has the most incredible drumming tradition. The Royal Drummers of Burundi are world-famous. Lake Tanganyika, the second deepest lake on Earth, forms the western border. Lake Tanganyika is so deep (4,823 feet) that it contains 18% of the world's available fresh water. The drumming tradition is so central that it is a UNESCO cultural heritage.

LVA.letter: Sveiki! Latvia has a song festival where 30,000 people sing together at once. The capital Riga has the most Art Nouveau buildings of any city in the world. Latvians are some of the tallest people on Earth. During the Soviet era, Latvians formed a human chain of 2 MILLION people stretching across three countries to protest for freedom. It worked.

MDA.letter: Bună ziua! Moldova has the world's largest wine cellar, Mileștii Mici, with underground tunnels stretching 200 KILOMETERS. That's enough wine to fill a couple million bottles. Wine is so important here it has its own national holiday. The wine tunnels of Mileștii Mici are so long that you drive through them in a car. It is literally an underground city of wine, complete with street names.

MKD.letter: Zdravo! North Macedonia has Lake Ohrid, one of the oldest and deepest lakes in Europe, over 3 million years old. Mother Teresa was born in Skopje, the capital. The government built hundreds of statues to make the city look like ancient Rome. Lake Ohrid is one of the oldest lakes in Europe and has species found nowhere else. Skopje was rebuilt with bizarre neoclassical statues everywhere after a campaign that locals love to argue about.

MNE.letter: Zdravo! Montenegro means 'Black Mountain.' The Bay of Kotor looks like a fjord and is one of the most beautiful coastlines in Europe. This tiny country only became independent in 2006, making it one of Europe's newest nations. The Tara River Canyon is the deepest in Europe. Ziplines cross over it. The old town of Kotor has a fortress wall that climbs 1,200 steps up a mountain.

AND.letter: Hola! Andorra is a tiny country squeezed between France and Spain in the Pyrenees mountains. It has no airport, no train station, and no military. Shopping is tax-free so everyone comes here to buy stuff cheap. Every winter, 8 MILLION tourists visit for skiing and tax-free shopping. That is 100 times the country's population.

VAT.letter: Salve! Vatican City is the SMALLEST country in the world. The entire country fits inside one neighborhood of Rome. The Pope lives here. The Sistine Chapel ceiling, painted by Michelangelo, is one of the greatest artworks ever. The Vatican has its own post office, its own radio station, and its own army (the Swiss Guard, who still dress in Renaissance-era uniforms designed by Michelangelo).

MCO.letter: Bonjour! Monaco is the second smallest country in the world but the RICHEST per person. The Formula 1 race goes through actual city streets. More millionaires live here per square foot than anywhere on Earth. The country is so small that the famous Formula 1 race literally goes through a tunnel, past yachts, and around hairpin turns between apartment buildings.

SMR.letter: Ciao! San Marino claims to be the OLDEST republic in the world, founded in 301 AD by a stonemason hiding from Roman persecution. It is completely surrounded by Italy and sits on top of Mount Titano. The three towers on the mountain are on the flag and the most iconic sight. San Marino has two heads of state at a time (Captains Regent) who serve for just SIX MONTHS each. That means the country gets new leaders twice a year. It has more cars than people and one of the highest GDPs per capita in the world.

LIE.letter: Hoi! Liechtenstein is so small that you can run across the ENTIRE COUNTRY in a few hours. People actually do this as a race. It sits between Switzerland and Austria. You can rent the entire country for a private event (about $70,000 a night). Despite being tiny, it has more registered companies than people because of low taxes. Liechtenstein has no army and the last time it went to war (in 1866), it sent out 80 soldiers and came back with 81 because they made a friend along the way. True story.

MLT.letter: Bongu! Malta has temples that are OLDER than the pyramids of Egypt AND Stonehenge. The Megalithic Temples were built around 3600 BC. The Knights of Malta once ruled this island fortress and their influence is everywhere. Almost every building is made of golden limestone that glows orange in the sunset. Malta is tiny but has been invaded and ruled by the Phoenicians, Romans, Arabs, Normans, French, and British. The Maltese language is the only Semitic language written in Latin letters, a mix of Arabic, Italian, and English.

BHS.letter: Hey! The Bahamas has SWIMMING PIGS. Wild pigs paddle out to boats for snacks on Big Major Cay. The water is so clear you can see the bottom even when it's 20 feet deep. There are 700 islands and most are uninhabited. The Bahamas gets its turquoise color from white sand and shallow water that reflects sunlight. Dean's Blue Hole is the deepest underwater sinkhole on Earth at 663 feet.

BRB.letter: Hey! Barbados is where Rihanna is from. The beaches have pink and white sand. Rum was basically invented here. Bajans (Barbadians) are incredibly proud and the island became a republic in 2021, removing the British crown. Mount Gay Rum has been produced here since 1703, making it the oldest commercial rum brand in the world. The rum punch recipe is a national secret everyone knows.

BLZ.letter: Hello! Belize has the Great Blue Hole, a giant underwater sinkhole over 400 feet deep that is visible from SPACE. Jacques Cousteau called it one of the top dive sites in the world. Ancient Maya temples poke up through the jungle, and Belize has more Maya ruins per square mile than almost anywhere. It is the only English-speaking country in Central America. The barrier reef off the coast is the second largest in the world (after Australia's). Howler monkeys are so loud you can hear them from 3 miles away. The national animal is the tapir, which looks like a pig crossed with an elephant.

FJI.letter: Bula! Fiji has over 330 islands surrounded by crystal-clear turquoise water. Fijians are famous for being some of the friendliest people on Earth. Rugby is the national obsession and Fiji's sevens team is legendary. Kava is the national drink. It looks like muddy water, tastes like dirt, and makes your mouth go numb. Fijians drink it at every social gathering and you WILL be offered some.

MDV.letter: Assalaamu alaikum! The Maldives is the flattest country on Earth. The highest point is only 5 feet above sea level. The water is so clear and blue it doesn't look real. Bioluminescent plankton makes the beaches glow at night. Some resorts here have underwater restaurants where you eat dinner while sharks and manta rays swim past your table.

BRN.letter: Selamat datang! Brunei is one of the richest countries in the world thanks to oil. The Sultan lives in the world's LARGEST residential palace with 1,788 rooms. Education and healthcare are free for everyone. The Sultan's palace has 257 bathrooms. TWO HUNDRED AND FIFTY-SEVEN. The royal collection includes over 7,000 cars.

BTN.letter: Kuzu zangpo! Bhutan measures success by Gross National HAPPINESS, not money. This mountain kingdom between India and China has no traffic lights in its entire capital city. A police officer directs traffic by hand and does it with so much style that tourists come just to watch. TV and internet only arrived in 1999! Bhutan is the ONLY carbon-negative country on Earth, which means its forests absorb more carbon than the country produces. Tiger's Nest Monastery clings to a cliff 3,000 feet above the valley floor.

KGZ.letter: Salam! Kyrgyzstan is 90% mountains with nomadic herders who still live in yurts, ride horses across vast grasslands, and hunt with golden eagles. The World Nomad Games are held here, a kind of Olympics for horseback sports, wrestling, and eagle hunting. Issyk-Kul is one of the largest alpine lakes in the world and it never freezes despite being surrounded by snow-capped peaks. The country had multiple revolutions in the 2000s and 2010s. Kyrgyz people are incredibly hospitable and will invite a stranger into their yurt for tea without hesitation.

TLS.letter: Bondia! Timor-Leste (East Timor) is one of the youngest countries in the world, gaining independence in 2002. The coral reefs here are some of the most biodiverse on Earth. Coffee grows in the mountains. The resistance fighters who won independence are national heroes. Timor-Leste is rebuilding from scratch. Independence Day celebrations on May 20 are massive. The resistance fighters who won freedom from Indonesia after 24 years of occupation are treated as living legends.

SYC.letter: Bonzour! The Seychelles has beaches so beautiful they look computer-generated. Granite boulders the size of houses sit on white sand with crystal water. Giant Aldabra tortoises live here and can weigh over 500 pounds and live for over 100 years. The coco de mer palm grows the largest seed in the entire plant kingdom, weighing up to 55 pounds. Only about 100,000 people live in the Seychelles, making it the smallest country in Africa by population. The country was uninhabited until about 250 years ago.

MUS.letter: Bonzour! Mauritius has an 'underwater waterfall' illusion visible from the air. The dodo bird lived here (and went extinct here). The seven-colored earth of Chamarel has sand dunes in seven distinct colors. The seven-colored earth of Chamarel never erodes into a uniform color no matter how much rain falls. Scientists still argue about why.

COM.letter: Bariza! Comoros is called the 'Perfume Islands' because ylang-ylang flowers grow everywhere and are used in the world's most expensive perfumes (Chanel No. 5 uses Comorian ylang-ylang). This tiny island nation floats between Madagascar and Africa. The presidency rotates between the three main islands so everyone gets a turn. A coelacanth, a fish scientists thought had been extinct for 65 MILLION YEARS, was found alive in Comorian waters. It was like finding a living dinosaur in your backyard.

STP.letter: Bom dia! São Tomé and Príncipe are two tiny volcanic islands off the coast of Africa. Some of the world's finest chocolate comes from cocoa grown here. The islands are so remote and pristine they feel like a secret. The islands straddle the equator, and there is a spot where you can literally stand in both hemispheres at once.

CPV.letter: Morabeza! Cabo Verde (Cape Verde) is a chain of volcanic islands in the Atlantic Ocean. The music here, especially morna, is hauntingly beautiful. Cesária Évora, the 'Barefoot Diva,' made Cabo Verdean music world-famous. More Cabo Verdeans live OUTSIDE Cabo Verde than inside it. The diaspora is spread across the world but everyone comes home for carnival.

KNA.letter: Saint Kitts and Nevis is the SMALLEST country in the entire Americas. A scenic railway circles the whole island. You can literally ride a train around an entire COUNTRY in one afternoon. The volcanic peaks are covered in rainforest. Alexander Hamilton, whose face is on the US $10 bill, was born on the island of Nevis. The whole island is basically one volcano.

LCA.letter: Bonjou! Saint Lucia has the Pitons, two dramatic volcanic peaks that rise straight out of the Caribbean Sea like giant green pyramids. This small island has produced TWO Nobel Prize winners from a population of only 180,000. That is more Nobel Prizes per capita than any country on Earth. You can literally drive into a volcano here (the Sulphur Springs are a drive-in volcanic crater). The food mixes French, African, and Caribbean traditions. Bouyon, a thick stew, is the comfort food everyone craves.

VCT.letter: St. Vincent and the Grenadines has 32 islands and PIRATES OF THE CARIBBEAN was filmed here. The black sand beaches come from volcanic rock. La Soufriere volcano erupted in 2021 and people had to evacuate by CRUISE SHIP. When La Soufrière erupted in 2021, cruise ships evacuated residents. People brought their animals on board. Goats on cruise ships. Real event.

DMA.letter: Hello from the Nature Isle! Dominica (not the Dominican Republic!) has a BOILING LAKE. The water bubbles at near 200 degrees because volcanic heat from below keeps it cooking. The island has 365 rivers, one for every day of the year. Dominica has the most untouched rainforest left in the Caribbean and the air smells like rain and flowers everywhere you go. The Sisserou parrot, found only here, is on the flag. Dominica is one of the few places where the indigenous Kalinago people still live on their ancestral land.
